phrases.text_local: Mají dárkové karty IGY datum spotřeby?
phrases.cultural_note: Czech gift cards typically have a set validity period from activation, stated clearly.

likely_response.translation_en: Valid for twelve months from activation.

responses.english: Valid for twelve months from activation.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how food court card works
phrases.text_local: Jak funguje karta do food courtu?
phrases.cultural_note: Prepaid cards are common in Czech food courts; 'dobít' means to top up or recharge.

likely_response.local: Dobijete ji a pak platíte u stánků.
likely_response.translation_en: You top it up and then pay at the stalls.

responses.english: You top it up and then pay at the stalls.
responses.french: Dobijete ji a pak platíte u stánků.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if parking validation still works
phrases.cultural_note: A simple 'Ano' (Yes) followed by the location is the standard direct answer in Czech service interactions.

likely_response.translation_en: Yes, at the machine by the elevators.

responses.english: Yes, at the machine by the elevators.

phrases.cultural_note: Czech shopping centers often use 'přízemí' for the ground floor, distinct from 'první patro' which is the first floor above ground.

likely_response.local: V přízemí, vlevo od hlavního vchodu.
likely_response.translation_en: On the ground floor, to the left of the main entrance.

responses.english: On the ground floor, to the left of the main entrance.
responses.french: V přízemí, vlevo od hlavního vchodu.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where CineStar ticket counter is
phrases.cultural_note: Czech directions often use 'úplně' (completely/all the way) for emphasis at the end of a hallway.

likely_response.translation_en: Upstairs by the escalators, all the way to the right.

responses.english: Upstairs by the escalators, all the way to the right.
